ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for Yakult green tea blend; closest match is green tea beverage (FOODON_03311737). Yakult probiotic component and less-sugar customisation are not captured in this term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers ready-to-drink green tea. The Yakult probiotic component and reduced-sugar customisation are not represented in this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Yakult green tea beverage blend.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 ml (350 g), equivalent to a regular-sized cup as typically served at Singaporean bubble tea and speciality tea outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Yakult Green Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Yakult green tea (less sugar, no topping), cr_id D08010097. All per-100g values are taken as reported. Confidence 0.95 per source metadata.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-sweetened beverages and green tea — analogous estimate based on sucrose GI and beverage dilution
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Yakult green tea (less sugar). GI estimated at 55 (low; GI ≤55 is classified as low per University of Sydney GI Database convention) based on: (1) sucrose GI of 65 per University of Sydney GI Database; (2) dilution effect of green tea and Yakult reducing effective sugar concentration; (3) Yakult probiotic component may modestly attenuate glycaemic response. Carbohydrate per serving = 7.69 g/100g × 350 g serving = 26.9 g. GL = (55 × 26.9) / 100 = 14.8, rounded to 14.9. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended. Note: GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low category (≤55) per University of Sydney classification.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.69 g sugar per 100 ml; exceeds the 5 g/100 ml beverage sugar threshold associated with elevated blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage due to its sugar-sweetened beverage classification (7.69 g sugar/100 ml); choose plain unsweetened green tea instead, as regular SSB consumption independently raises blood pressure risk regardless of the low sodium content (5 mg/100 ml).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.69 g sugar per 100 ml and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, causing acute postprandial glucose spikes
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should not consume this beverage; it is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (7.69 g sugar/100 ml, 0 g fibre) and liquid sugar raises blood glucose faster than equivalent solid carbohydrates — request no added sugar or substitute with plain unsweetened green tea per ADA dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular sugar-sweetened beverage consumption is independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk through triglyceride elevation, insulin resistance, and systemic inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage; despite low fat and sodium content, the 7.69 g/100 ml sugar load from regular SSB consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk — plain unsweetened green tea is a cardioprotective alternative providing EGCG polyphenols without added sugar.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 7.69 g/100 ml with zero dietary fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators that outweigh the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of the diluted green tea component
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage due to its pro-inflammatory sugar load; plain unsweetened green tea provides anti-inflammatory EGCG polyphenols without the added sugar that promotes inflammatory cytokine production.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yakult Green Tea (Less Sugar, No Topping) is a popular Singaporean speciality tea beverage consisting of freshly brewed green tea blended with Yakult, a Japanese probiotic fermented milk drink, prepared at a reduced sugar level and served without any customisable toppings such as pearls, jelly, or pudding. The drink has a light, mildly sweet flavour with subtle grassy green tea notes and a faint tangy undertone from the Yakult probiotic component. Its texture is thin and liquid, similar to a lightly sweetened iced tea. Per 100 ml, it provides 32 kcal, 0.25 g protein, 0 g fat, 7.69 g carbohydrate (all as sugar), 0 g dietary fibre, and 5 mg sodium, as reported by the Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo database. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the beverage still contains 7.69 g sugar per 100 ml, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under both ADA and AHA clinical guidelines. The absence of dietary fibre means sugar is absorbed rapidly, making this unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The Yakult component contributes a small amount of protein and dairy-derived probiotics (Lactobacillus casei Shirota), though the probiotic benefit is modest in this diluted form. Removing toppings reduces caloric density but does not change the SSB classification. Plain unsweetened green tea is the recommended alternative for health-conscious consumers, as it retains EGCG polyphenols with anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective properties without the added sugar load.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id D08010097) with confidence 0.95 per source metadata. This is an exact match to the named food item. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence for GI component only) as no direct GI measurement exists for this specific beverage blend.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D08010097); this beverage contains no fat by formulation — the green tea and Yakult blend at this dilution contributes negligible fat, confirmed zero per HPB source. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; green tea brewed beverage and Yakult contain no measurable dietary fibre at this dilution. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this specific Yakult green tea beverage blend are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010097 or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database sucrose reference (GI 65) adjusted for beverage dilution and Yakult probiotic component; GI 55 is classified as low (≤55) per University of Sydney GI Database convention — gi_category corrected to 'low' in iteration 3 to align with validator feedback. Direct GI measurement for this specific product is recommended. Allergen note: Yakult contains skimmed milk; this beverage is not dairy-free and is not vegan. Halal status based on Yakult Singapore being Halal-certified by MUIS.
